---
title: "The Social Psychology of Democracies in Transition"
---

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/sync-teaching.py from the Teaching repo.
     Do not edit by hand — re-run build.sh (or the script) to regenerate. -->

<p class="teaching-back"><a href="../teaching.html">← All courses</a></p>

## Slides

1. [Introduction](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-1-slides)
2. [Transition to (and from?) democracy: an overview](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-2-slides)
3. [Legacies of communism](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-3-slides)
4. [Social-psychological theories and communist legacies](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-4-slides)
5. [The self under communism and the self after communism](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-5-slides)
6. [Attitudes, values, orientations and beliefs](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-6-slides)
7. [Social influence and social norms](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-7-slides)
8. [Anti-social behaviour: anomie and aggression](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-8-slides)
9. [Pro-social behaviour: helping and altruism](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-9-slides)
10. [Groups and group dynamics](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-10-slides)
11. [Prejudice and intergroup relations (1): the nature of post-communist prejudice](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-11-slides)
12. [Prejudice and intergroup relations (2): reducing prejudice](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-12-slides)
13. [Cultural social psychology: intercultural relations](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-13-slides)
14. [The psychological underpinnings of democracy and democratic backsliding](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-14-slides)
15. [Course synthesis and exam preparation](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-15-slides)

## Handouts

- [Introduction](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-1-handout)
- [Transition to (and from?) democracy: an overview](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-2-handout)
- [Legacies of communism](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-3-handout)
- [Social-psychological theories and communist legacies](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-4-handout)
- [The self under communism and the self after communism](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-5-handout)
- [Attitudes, values, orientations and beliefs](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-6-handout)
- [Social influence and social norms](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-7-handout)
- [Anti-social behaviour: anomie and aggression](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-8-handout)
- [Pro-social behaviour: helping and altruism](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-9-handout)
- [Groups and group dynamics](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-10-handout)
- [Prejudice and intergroup relations (1): the nature of post-communist prejudice](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-11-handout)
- [Prejudice and intergroup relations (2): reducing prejudice](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-12-handout)
- [Cultural social psychology: intercultural relations](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-13-handout)
- [The psychological underpinnings of democracy and democratic backsliding](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-14-handout)
- [Course synthesis and exam preparation](https://bdstanley.netlify.app/social-psychology-democracies-15-handout)
